name: What questions should I ask a general contractor before I hire them?
headline: What questions should I ask a general contractor before I hire them?

mainEntity.name: What questions should I ask a general contractor before I hire them?
mainEntity.text: What questions should I ask a general contractor before I hire them?

acceptedAnswer.text: General contractors expect you to have questions, and the right ones can save you time and money. Before you hire someone in Pacifica, Daly City, or nearby, ask: - Are you licensed and insured, and what is your license number? - How many projects like mine have you done in this area? - Can I see recent local references and photos? - Who will be on site every day, and who is my main contact? - Do you handle permits and inspections? - What is the estimated timeline for my project? - How do you handle changes and unexpected problems? - What does your payment schedule look like? - Do you offer a warranty on your work, and for how long? Listen closely to how they answer. Are they clear and patient, or do they rush you? Do they explain things in a way you understand? A common mistake is feeling embarrassed to ask basic questions or to push for details in writing. Remember, this is a major investment, and part of a contractor’s job is to communicate clearly. Write your questions down before the meeting, and take notes during the conversation so you can compare contractors later. If someone gets annoyed by your questions, that’s usually a sign to move on.
